[607]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蘭岩曰 - Classical expression meaning 'Lan Yan said,' introducing a commentary or reflection.
  • 诵咒戒牛 - Literally 'reciting incantations to warn the ox,' a metaphorical or allegorical phrase that requires contextual interpretation.
  • 得免于难 - Means 'to escape from difficulty' or 'to avoid disaster.'
  • 酒能误事 - Literally 'wine can cause trouble,' a common Chinese saying indicating that alcohol can lead to mistakes or mishaps.
  • 此人独以大醉免死 - Means 'this person alone was spared from death by being drunk.'
  • 岂巨人亦惧其酒狂耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'could even a giant fear his wine-induced madness?'
  • 抑醉亦为冥间所弃耶 - Another rhetorical question meaning 'or is drunkenness also abandoned by the underworld?'
  • 白莲教 - Refers to the White Lotus Society, a secret religious organization with a history of rebellion against the Qing dynasty.
  • 京山富人许翁 - Refers to a wealthy man named Xu from Jing Shan, a place name.
  • 世居皂市阳桑湖畔 - Means 'lived for generations by the Yang Sang Lake in Zao Shi.'
  • 为其子娶妇 - Means 'arranged a marriage for his son.'
  • 乡宦而富豪者 - Refers to a local official and wealthy person.
  • 妆奁丰厚 - Means 'the dowry was rich and abundant.'
  • 一乡之所艳羡 - Means 'envied by the entire village.'
  • 偷儿杨三 - Refers to a thief named Yang San.
  • 觊觎半年 - Means 'coveted for half a year.'
  • 拔贡 - Refers to a type of scholar-official selected through the imperial examination system.
  • 肄业成均 - Means 'to study at the Imperial Academy.'
  • 图进取 - Means 'to strive for advancement.'
  • 俟进行 - Means 'waiting for the journey to begin.'
  • 夜入内室 - Means 'to enter the inner room at night.'
  • 伏暗处俟之 - Means 'to hide in the dark and wait for him.'
  • 新妇方娠 - Means 'the new wife was pregnant.'
  • 不耐久坐 - Means 'unable to sit for long.'
  • 二更即寝 - Means 'went to bed at the second watch of the night.'
  • 相伴唯二婢 - Means 'accompanied only by two maids.'
  • 就灯作灯黹 - Means 'working by the lamp on needlework.'
  • 始闭户亦各谋睡 - Means 'finally closed the door and each went to sleep.'
  • 移灯至几上 - Means 'moved the lamp to the table.'
  • 光明如昼 - Means 'as bright as day.'
  • 闻鼾声 - Means 'heard snoring.'
  • 知已睡熟 - Means 'knew he was asleep.'
  • 方欲窃发 - Means 'just about to steal something.'
  • 蓦见房门自开 - Means 'suddenly saw the door open by itself.'
  • 一人启帘入 - Means 'a person entered by opening the curtain.'
  • 深目耸鼻 - Means 'deep-set eyes and a prominent nose.'
  • 黑须绕颊 - Means 'black beard around the cheeks.'
  • 背负黄布囊 - Means 'carrying a yellow cloth bag on his back.'
  • 狞恶殊可怖 - Means 'fierce and ugly, very frightening.'
  • 阴念 - Means 'thought secretly.'
  • 道中未见此人 - Means 'had never seen this person before.'
  • 必有诡异 - Means 'must be something strange.'
  • 姑屏息蝟缩 - Means 'temporarily held his breath and crouched down.'
  • 以觇其所为 - Means 'to observe what he was doing.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兰岩 - A possible pseudonym or title, but without additional context, it remains unclear.
  • 白莲教 - The White Lotus Society, a secretive religious group with a history of rebellion against the Qing dynasty.
  • 京山 - A place name in Hubei Province, China.
  • 拔贡 - A type of scholar-official selected through the imperial examination system.
  • 成均 - The Imperial Academy, a prestigious educational institution during the imperial era.
  • 二更 - The second watch of the night, typically from 9 PM to 11 PM.
  • 灯黹 - Needlework done by the light of a lamp.
  • 冥间 - The underworld, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 诵咒戒牛，得免于难 - This phrase is elliptical and requires contextual interpretation. It could mean that reciting incantations to warn the ox helped avoid a disaster.
  • 此人独以大醉免死 - The sentence structure is compact and requires careful reading to understand the sequence of events.
  • 岂巨人亦惧其酒狂耶，抑醉亦为冥间所弃耶 - This compound rhetorical question is grammatically complex and requires careful parsing.
  • 为其子娶妇，亦乡宦而富豪者 - The sentence structure is elliptical and requires careful reading to understand the relationship between the clauses.
  • 俟进行，而夜入内室 - The sequence of actions is compact and requires careful reading to understand the timing and sequence of events.
  • 蓦见房门自开，一人启帘入 - The sentence structure is compact and requires careful reading to understand the sequence of events.

Proper Nouns

  • 兰岩 - A possible pseudonym or title, but without additional context, it remains unclear.
  • 白莲教 - The White Lotus Society.
  • 京山 - A place name in Hubei Province, China.
  • 许翁 - A wealthy man named Xu.
  • 杨三 - A thief named Yang San.
  • 拔贡 - A type of scholar-official selected through the imperial examination system.
  • 成均 - The Imperial Academy.
  • 二更 - The second watch of the night.
  • 灯黹 - Needlework done by the light of a lamp.
  • 冥间 - The underworld.

[608]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鹗顾 - Metaphor for a sharp, suspicious glance, often used in classical literature to describe someone who is wary or distrustful.
  • 花睡 - Metaphor for deep sleep, comparing it to a flower in full bloom, often used in classical literature to describe a peaceful or serene state.
  • 戟指 - Gesture of pointing with a finger in a threatening manner, often used in classical literature to describe someone who is angry or hostile.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 香 - Incense is often used in classical Chinese literature to create a ritualistic or ceremonial atmosphere, or to symbolize purification or communication with the divine.
  • 罗帐 - Silk curtain, often used in classical literature to create a private or ceremonial space, or to symbolize luxury and refinement.
  • 绣衾 - Embroidered quilt, often used in classical literature to symbolize warmth, comfort, or luxury.
  • 喃喃 - Muttering, often used in classical literature to describe someone who is speaking in a low, incoherent voice, possibly in a curse or incantation.

[609]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惊怕忿恨 - Combines fear, anger, and resentment, describing a complex emotional state.
  • 盗念顿灰 - Literally 'theft thoughts suddenly turned to ashes,' a metaphor for the sudden loss of criminal intent or desire.
  • 密观其所经 - Means 'secretly observe where he goes,' indicating covert surveillance.
  • 洞开无阻 - Literally 'open like a cave with no obstruction,' describing something that is easily accessible or unobstructed.
  • 寄迹之所 - Means 'a place of temporary residence,' often used to describe a place where someone stays for a short time.
  • 伪作行客 - Means 'pretending to be a traveler,' indicating someone who is not what they seem.
  • 聊憩檐下 - Means 'to rest briefly under the eaves,' describing a temporary stop.
  • 坐以待旦 - Means 'to sit and wait for dawn,' indicating patience and vigilance.
  • 客请少停 - Means 'please stay a moment,' a polite request.
  • 有密事举白 - Means 'there is a secret matter to report,' indicating a serious and confidential issue.
  • 饰说 - Means 'to embellish one's words,' describing attempts to deceive or mislead.
  • 检其布囊 - Means 'to check his cloth bag,' suggesting a search for evidence.
  • 证据 - Means 'evidence,' a term that requires careful handling to maintain its meaning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 四川 - Refers to Sichuan, a province in southwestern China.
  • 江南 - Refers to the southern part of China, particularly the region south of the Yangtze River.
  • 妖人 - Refers to a sorcerer or someone with supernatural abilities, often used in a negative context.
  • 蜡客 - Refers to a person involved in the production or trade of wax, possibly a craftsman or merchant.
  • 旅店 - Refers to an inn or hotel, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨 - The name of the narrator, which should be consistently translated as 'Yang.'
  • 四川 - The place name Sichuan, which should be consistently translated as 'Sichuan.'
  • 江南 - The place name Jiangnan, which should be consistently translated as 'Jiangnan.'
  • 妖人 - The term for sorcerer, which should be consistently translated as 'sorcerer' or 'supernatural being.'

[610]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 众是之 - Colloquial expression meaning 'everyone agreed' or 'everyone approved.'
  • 开囊聚观 - Literally 'opened the bag and gathered to look,' describes the act of opening a bag and showing its contents to a group.
  • 累累然 - Describes something as 'accumulated' or 'stacked up,' often with a sense of abundance.
  • 惶遽 - Means 'frightened' or 'panicked.'
  • 奈何扰攘 - Rhetorical question meaning 'why disturb' or 'what for to disturb.'
  • 谁劫汝财 - Rhetorical question meaning 'who is robbing your money.'
  • 无事出言伤众 - Means 'to speak without cause and harm others.'
  • 显有情弊 - Means 'clearly showing some ulterior motive' or 'evidently having some hidden agenda.'
  • 挺身奋出 - Means 'stood up and stepped forward boldly.'
  • 夺一罐开之 - Means 'took one jar and opened it.'
  • 腥气触鼻 - Means 'the smell of blood was overwhelming.'
  • 倾视 - Means 'poured out and looked at.'
  • 尽小儿心肝 - Means 'all the hearts and livers of children.'
  • 尚空三罐 - Means 'still three jars were empty.'
  • 骇异 - Means 'shocked and amazed.'
  • 致诘那得此物 - Means 'to ask how one could obtain such things.'
  • 彼必不承 - Means 'he will surely not admit.'
  • 请以代白 - Means 'please let me speak on his behalf.'
  • 因述夜间之事 - Means 'then recounted the events of the night.'
  • 大动公忿 - Means 'greatly stirred public anger.'
  • 竞挥老拳 - Means 'everyone started hitting him with their fists.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 罐中黄白 - Refers to gold and silver, often used to describe wealth or treasure.
  • 一生衣食之本 - Means 'the fundamental means of livelihood for one's entire life.'
  • 纣以天下之尊 - Refers to King Zhou of Shang, the last king of the Shang dynasty, known for his tyranny.
  • 刳剖孕妇 - Means 'to disembowel pregnant women,' a reference to King Zhou's cruel acts.
  • 破卵伤胎 - Means 'to break eggs and harm fetuses,' a metaphor for causing harm to innocent lives.
  • 上天好生 - Means 'the benevolence of heaven,' suggesting divine protection or mercy.
  • 假手宵人 - Means 'to use the hands of night people,' a euphemism for using criminals or evildoers.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨 - The name of the person who recounted the events.

[611]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 主人恐其致毙 - The host fears that the situation will lead to death.
  • 方欲止之 - Is about to stop it.
  • 瞑目大叱 - Closes his eyes and roars loudly.
  • 如触木石 - As if hitting wood or stone.
  • 指节损破 - Fingertips are damaged or broken.
  • 仓卒间急提一罐 - In a hurry, quickly grabs a jar.
  • 自其人头上倾之 - Pours it over the person's head.
  • 连作恨声 - Continuously makes sounds of regret.
  • 罢了！罢了！莫非数也 - It's over! It's over! Is it fate?
  • 小不忍则乱大谋 - A small act of impatience can ruin a great plan.
  • 倘打坏，谁任其咎 - If we hurt him, who will be responsible?
  • 不如执之送县 - It would be better to take him to the county.
  • 自有国法在 - There are national laws to follow.
  • 听官断可也 - Let the official judge it.
  • 许家男妇已在 - The men and women of the Xu family are already there.
  • 杨更述之 - Yang recounts it.
  • 吾不忍复至公庭 - I cannot bear to go to the public court again.
  • 致宦家闺秀，暴露尸骸也 - To expose the body of a noblewoman.
  • 感其言 - Are moved by his words.
  • 相与驱车而返 - Drive away together.
  • 县宰细讯得实 - The county magistrate carefully interrogates and finds the truth.
  • 方知为白莲教妖人之党 - Only then realizes they are part of the White Lotus Sect's heretical group.
  • 取小儿心肝者 - Those who take the hearts and livers of children.
  • 亦行持邪术必需之物也 - Are also necessary for practicing heretical magic.
  • 时湘汉一带胎妇被剖者甚多 - At the time, many pregnant women in the Xiang and Han regions were being cut open.
  • 至此始得其故 - Only now do we understand the reason.
  • 并得其党名姓面貌数十人 - And find the names, faces, and appearances of dozens of their accomplices.
  • 陆续捕获 - Capture them one by one.
  • 寸磔其人于市 - Dissect the person in the market square.
  • 杨杖二十，给银五十两 - Yang receives twenty strokes of the cane and fifty taels of silver.
  • 责其为盗而赏其捉奸也 - Punished for being a thief but rewarded for catching a wrongdoer.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 白莲教 - Refers to the White Lotus Sect, a religious and political movement in Chinese history.
  • 胎妇被剖 - Refers to the practice of cutting open pregnant women, a reference to historical atrocities.
  • 寸磔 - Refers to the ancient punishment of dissection, often used for heretics or criminals.
  • 县宰 - Refers to the county magistrate, a local government official.
  • 国法 - Refers to the national laws of the time.
  • 公庭 - Refers to the public court, a place of legal proceedings.
  • 宦家闺秀 - Refers to a noblewoman, a member of a high-ranking family.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The host fears that the situation will lead to death and is about to stop it. The person suddenly closes his eyes and roars loudly; the crowd punches him, as if hitting wood or stone, damaging his fingertips. The host is greatly alarmed and, in a hurry, quickly grabs a jar and pours it over the person's head. The person continuously makes sounds of regret, saying, 'It's over! It's over! Is it fate?' The crowd beats him again, and the host says, 'A small act of impatience can ruin a great plan. If we hurt him, who will be responsible?' It would be better to take him to the county, there are national laws to follow, let the official judge it. The men and women of the Xu family are already there, Yang recounts it. I cannot bear to go to the public court again, to expose the body of a noblewoman. Are moved by his words, and all stop the lawsuit, drive away together. The county magistrate carefully interrogates and finds the truth, only then realizes they are part of the White Lotus Sect's heretical group. Those who take the hearts and livers of children, are also necessary for practicing heretical magic. At the time, many pregnant women in the Xiang and Han regions were being cut open, only now do we understand the reason, and find the names, faces, and appearances of dozens of their accomplices, capture them one by one. Dissect the person in the market square, Yang receives twenty strokes of the cane and fifty taels of silver, punished for being a thief but rewarded for catching a wrongdoer.

Proper Nouns

  • 主人 - The host.
  • 许家 - The Xu family.
  • 杨 - Yang.
  • 县宰 - The county magistrate.
  • 白莲教 - The White Lotus Sect.
  • 湘汉一带 - The Xiang and Han regions.
  • 宦家闺秀 - A noblewoman.
  • 国法 - National laws.
  • 公庭 - The public court.

[612]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妖術杀人 - Compound phrase meaning 'killing through witchcraft' or 'murder by supernatural means.'
  • 惨酷已极 - Translates to 'extremely cruel and merciless' or 'reaching the peak of cruelty.'
  • 固天人之所共愤者 - Formal expression meaning 'certainly something that heaven and humanity both resent.'
  • 假手宵人 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to use the hands of villains' or 'to let villains act on one's behalf.'
  • 以败其事 - 'To ruin the matter' or 'to bring about the failure of the plan.'
  • 抑亦巧矣 - Rhetorical expression meaning 'yet it is also a clever move' or 'but it is also a shrewd strategy.'
  • 兴讼结仇 - 'To initiate lawsuits and create enmity.'
  • 多人牵累 - 'To involve many people' or 'to entangle many individuals.'
  • 痛雪新妇之冤哉 - 'To thoroughly clear the bride's injustice' or 'to completely vindicate the bride's wrong.'
  • 鬼哭贵阳太守某公之母 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'the ghost wailed for the mother of a certain governor of Guiyang.'
  • 病濒危 - 'Sick and near death' or 'on the brink of death.'
  • 亲戚邻里来候问者 - 'Relatives and neighbors who came to inquire or visit.'
  • 设酒馔于厅上款之 - 'Set up wine and food in the hall to entertain them.'
  • 二更始散去 - 'They began to disperse at the second watch.'
  • 余尚多，有子侄四五人，复聚饮于斋中 - 'There were still many left, and four or five nephews and cousins gathered to drink in the study.'
  • 三更后，忽有哭声 - 'After the third watch, suddenly there was a crying sound.'
  • 越北窗外，类少妇而音甚惨切 - 'Beyond the northern window, it sounded like a young woman, but the voice was very sorrowful.'
  • 举室惊默相向 - 'The whole household was stunned and silent, looking at each other.'
  • 毛戴 - 'Hair stood on end' or 'hair raised' – a classical idiom meaning to be terrified.
  • 咸知其为鬼也 - 'All knew it was a ghost.'
  • 一食顷 - 'In the time of a meal' or 'a short while.'
  • 闻宅内悲声群动 - 'Hearing the house filled with sorrowful cries and commotion.'
  • 家人奔走来告 - 'Family members ran to inform' or 'family members rushed to report.'
  • 太夫人已气绝矣 - 'The mother-in-law had already died.'
  • 俗谚有丧门吊客之说 - 'There is a common saying about 'death-door guests' or 'funeral visitors.'
  • 理或不诬也 - 'It may not be baseless' or 'the saying may not be without foundation.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妖术 - Refers to supernatural or magical practices, often associated with witchcraft or sorcery.
  • 天人之所共愤 - Classical expression combining 'heaven' and 'humankind,' implying a moral and cosmic judgment.
  • 宵人 - Classical term for 'people of the night' or 'evil-doers.'
  • 贵阳太守某公之母 - 'The mother of a certain governor of Guiyang.' '太守' is the title of a local governor or magistrate.
  • 设酒馔于厅上款之 - Reflects the traditional Chinese practice of hosting guests with food and wine.
  • 丧门吊客 - A folk belief in Chinese culture about people or events that bring death or misfortune.
  • 太夫人 - A respectful term for a mother-in-law or a high-ranking lady.

[613]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其事有之，其理不解 - The matter exists, but the principle is not understood, indicating a situation that is observable but lacks a clear explanation.
  • 幾於行乞 - Literally means 'almost begging,' describing a state of extreme poverty.
  • 萬不獲已 - Means 'absolutely unwilling but forced to,' indicating a situation of extreme necessity.
  • 以延殘喘 - Literally means 'to prolong one's last breath,' referring to barely surviving.
  • 肆主以為笑談 - Means 'the shop owner treated it as a joke,' indicating a dismissive attitude.
  • 置不理 - Means 'ignored it,' showing a lack of concern or action.
  • 恨我一时在困苦中耳 - Means 'I regret being in such hardship at the moment,' expressing frustration.
  • 苟有日发迹 - Means 'if I ever succeed in the future,' indicating a conditional statement about future success.
  • 誓亦开一解库 - Means 'I swear to open a pawnshop,' expressing a strong vow.
  • 彼时虽有人将死孩儿来质，亦必质之矣 - Means 'even if someone brings a dying child to pawn, I will still accept it,' indicating a commitment to accepting any item for pawn.
  • 店肆最忌质死孩儿之说 - Means 'pawnshops most detest the idea of pawning a dying child,' indicating a taboo in pawnshop practices.
  • 闻之颇不甘 - Means 'hearing this, he was quite displeased,' expressing dissatisfaction.
  • 第以其贫窭至极 - Means 'only because he is extremely poor,' indicating the reason for his reaction.
  • 不足与较 - Means 'not worth arguing about,' indicating a decision to let it go.
  • 故为隐忍 - Means 'so he endured it silently,' indicating a passive response.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 袁翁 - Refers to Mr. Yuan, a character in the story.
  • 袁翁长山袁翁 - Refers to Mr. Yuan from Longshan, indicating his place of origin.
  • 典肆 - Refers to a pawnshop, a common financial institution in ancient China.
  • 解库 - Refers to a pawnshop, same as above.
  • 质钱 - Refers to pawning items to obtain money, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 行乞 - Refers to begging, indicating extreme poverty.
  • 死孩儿 - Refers to a dying child, indicating a taboo in pawnshop practices.
  • 店肆 - Refers to a shop or store, same as above.
  • 质之 - Refers to pawning an item, same as above.
  • 隐忍 - Refers to enduring silently, indicating a passive response.

Proper Nouns

  • 袁翁 - Mr. Yuan, a character in the story.
  • 长山 - Longshan, indicating Mr. Yuan's place of origin.
  • 典肆 - Pawnshop, a common financial institution in ancient China.
  • 解库 - Pawnshop, same as above.
  • 质钱 - Pawning items to obtain money, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 行乞 - Begging, indicating extreme poverty.
  • 死孩儿 - Dying child, indicating a taboo in pawnshop practices.
  • 店肆 - Shop or store, same as above.
  • 解库 - Pawnshop, same as above.
  • 质之 - Pawning an item, same as above.
  • 隐忍 - Enduring silently, indicating a passive response.

[614]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翁归去 - Indicates the departure of a man (翁) who returns home or goes somewhere.
  • 一路冥想 - Literally 'meditating along the way,' meaning deep thought or contemplation during a journey.
  • 毫无生趣 - A phrase meaning 'no interest in life' or 'lifeless,' indicating a lack of enthusiasm or vitality.
  • 乃止步向天号泣 - A phrase meaning 'then stopped, turned to heaven, and wept aloud,' indicating a moment of emotional outburst.
  • 呜呼！袁某自问于心 - A phrase meaning 'Alas! Yuan awoke himself to the heart,' indicating a moment of introspection or self-reflection.
  • 所行之事，无不可告人者 - A phrase meaning 'the things I have done, none of which are shameful to confess,' indicating honesty and integrity.
  • 胡为而竟至于此耶 - A phrase meaning 'Why have I come to this state?' indicating a sense of despair or confusion.
  • 良久辍泣 - A phrase meaning 'after a long time, stopped crying,' indicating a pause in emotional expression.
  • 复行 - A phrase meaning 'resumed walking,' indicating a continuation of the journey.
  • 破衣为棘刺所牵 - A phrase meaning 'his tattered clothes were caught by thorns,' indicating a physical obstacle.
  • 猝难摆脱 - A phrase meaning 'suddenly difficult to escape,' indicating a sudden and challenging situation.
  • 屈身摘之 - A phrase meaning 'bent down to pick it up,' indicating a physical action.
  • 觉棘下土甚松 - A phrase meaning 'felt that the soil under the thorns was very loose,' indicating a sensory observation.
  • 试抄以手 - A phrase meaning 'tried to scoop it with his hand,' indicating a tentative action.
  • 土中有物累累然 - A phrase meaning 'there were objects piled up in the soil,' indicating a discovery.
  • 白光灿铄 - A phrase meaning 'white light shining brightly,' indicating a visual phenomenon.
  • 取视二枚 - A phrase meaning 'picked up and saw two pieces,' indicating a specific action.
  • 则朱提也 - A phrase meaning 'it turned out to be Zhu Ti,' indicating a realization.
  • 大惊喜 - A phrase meaning 'great surprise and joy,' indicating a positive emotional reaction.
  • 即以破衣裹数锭 - A phrase meaning 'immediately wrapped several ingots in his tattered clothes,' indicating a practical action.
  • 仍以土密掩其余者以归 - A phrase meaning 'still covered the rest with soil and returned home,' indicating a completion of a task.
  • 次晚，复往取之 - A phrase meaning 'the next evening, went back to take it,' indicating a repeated action.
  • 多不胜取 - A phrase meaning 'too many to take,' indicating an abundance.
  • 数旬方尽 - A phrase meaning 'after several weeks, it was all taken,' indicating a prolonged period.
  • 约略二万金 - A phrase meaning 'approximately two million coins,' indicating a large sum of money.
  • 不敢彰露 - A phrase meaning 'did not dare to show it,' indicating secrecy.
  • 先作些小生意 - A phrase meaning 'started with some small business,' indicating a modest beginning.
  • 逐渐张大 - A phrase meaning 'gradually expanded,' indicating growth.
  • 一年之后，遂为巨贾 - A phrase meaning 'after one year, became a wealthy merchant,' indicating success.
